allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian q1 mean-side REML only when derivation and tests name the route
forbidden_wording: non-Gaussian REML; q4 AI-REML; HSquared AI-REML
claim_boundary: REML wording is route-specific and exact-Gaussian only.
next_gate: Keep q1 mean-side separate from sigma and q4.

allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian pure-mu REML for unlabelled intercept or independent one-slope spatial terms with sigma ~ 1 and no sigma random effect
forbidden_wording: slope-only; labelled or multiple slopes; estimated range; matched mu+sigma; bivariate; non-Gaussian; nominal-exact or supported wording
claim_boundary: Inference-ready with caveats only at n_each=20 and M={8,16,32}; the fitted structured SD scale s_j gives covariance s_j^2 K_h, and node i has marginal SD s_j sqrt(K_h[ii]).
next_gate: Require separately reviewed evidence for every wider route or domain.

allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian pure-mu REML for unlabelled intercept or independent one-slope animal terms with sigma ~ 1 and no sigma random effect
forbidden_wording: slope-only; labelled or multiple slopes; large sparse pedigree; matched mu+sigma; bivariate; non-Gaussian; nominal-exact or supported wording
claim_boundary: Inference-ready with caveats only at n_each=20 on the fixed M=8 pedigree; A Ainv and pedigree parity is deterministic rather than campaign evidence.
next_gate: Require separately reviewed evidence for every wider route or domain.

allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian pure-mu REML for unlabelled intercept or independent one-slope relmat terms with sigma ~ 1 and no sigma random effect
forbidden_wording: slope-only; labelled or multiple slopes; arbitrary sparse or ill-conditioned precision; matched mu+sigma; bivariate; non-Gaussian; nominal-exact or supported wording
claim_boundary: Inference-ready with caveats only at n_each=20 and M={8,16,32}; K and Q parity is deterministic rather than campaign evidence.
next_gate: Require separately reviewed evidence for every wider route or domain.

allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian pure sigma-side phylogenetic REML for an intercept-only structured effect
forbidden_wording: inference-ready interval or broad sigma-side REML support inferred from an admission-level fit
claim_boundary: Native TMB pure sigma-side phylogenetic REML is implemented with point-fit admission evidence only; interval reliability, coverage, supported wording, and non-phylogenetic sigma-side transfer are not promoted.
next_gate: Add a retained multi-seed recovery and interval campaign before any inference-ready claim.

allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian phylogenetic q2 REML for the implemented matched univariate mu-sigma and bivariate mean-side layouts
forbidden_wording: interval reliability coverage supported wording or broad non-phylo q2 transfer inferred from admission tests
claim_boundary: Native phylogenetic q2 REML is implemented with row-specific point-fit recovery evidence; intervals, coverage, supported wording, and non-phylogenetic q2 transfer are not promoted.
next_gate: Add row-specific retained interval evidence before inference-ready wording.

allowed_wording: exact-Gaussian bivariate phylogenetic block-diagonal or dense q4 REML at the tested information scale
forbidden_wording: q4 native REML labelled HSquared AI-REML; interval reliability coverage or supported wording inferred from recovery
claim_boundary: Native TMB q4 REML is implemented with recovery evidence for the tested block-diagonal and dense layouts; it is not HSquared AI-REML and does not promote q4 interval reliability coverage or supported wording.
next_gate: Add calibrated q4 interval evidence before inference-ready promotion.

claim_boundary: Non-Gaussian structured rows do not use REML wording.

allowed_wording: experimental or planned bridge wording only after row-specific parity
forbidden_wording: public bridge REML support from direct Julia evidence or finite bridge admission alone
claim_boundary: Q1 sigma-only and q1 mu+sigma bridge REML admissions are live bridge evidence only; direct DRM.jl evidence is not broad R bridge support.
next_gate: Require native direct and R-via-Julia parity before support wording.
